channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: High-stakes AI summit in Paris: World leaders, tech titans and challenging diplomatic talks
item.description: Major world political leaders are meeting for an AI summit in Paris, where challenging diplomatic talks are expected while tech titans fight for dominance in the fast-moving technology industry.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk calls for annual firing of judges after blocked DOGE access at Treasury
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk called for the annual firing of judges following an early Saturday decision from a judge stating that the Treasury Department should bar access to its payment systems to anyone besides “civil servants with a need for access to perform their job duties.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Super Bowl 2025 LIVE: how to watch Eagles vs Chiefs online now, halftime show, live scores, odds, 3rd quarter
item.description: Eager to watch the Super Bowl? Here are the best streams, the cheapest prices and how to watch all four quarters live online as Patrick Mahomes' Kansas City face Philadelphia at New Orleans' Superdome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Bill would ban DeepSeek
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oppo Find N5: A Potential Game-Changer in the Foldable Market?
item.description: Oppo's upcoming Find N5 may outshine Samsung's Galaxy Z Fold 6, with improved waterproofing and a nearly invisible crease on its display.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: Java Development Kit (JDK) 24 has entered the release candidate stage. The next version of standard Java will ship with 24 features, ranging from a class-file API to a fourth preview of structured concurrency. The first JDK 24 release candidate arrived February 6, following two rampdown stages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Musk pledges to rehire young DOGE member after recent racist posts
item.description: The work DOGE has drawn fury from Democrats and charges that it is illegally accessing the private data of millions of Americans, and violating the Constitution.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Mistral's Le Chat Generative AI Assistant Now Available on Android and iOS
item.description: Mistral releases several updates to its generative AI assistant, Le Chat, making it available on Android and iOS platforms. The tool is described as a comprehensive genAI assistant for both life and work.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Bay Area Tech Companies Announce Thousands of Layoffs
item.description: Several tech companies in the Bay Area have revealed plans for a new round of layoffs, affecting thousands of technology jobs in the region.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers, District of Columbia Attorney General Brian L. Schwalb said Friday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Robot dog sprints into history books by breaking speed records
item.description: A Chinese startup has developed a robot, Mirror Me, that has run about 100 meters in under 10 seconds, pushing the boundaries of robotics and speed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Bay Area companies Salesforce, Workday are hiring more overseas to save cash
item.description: Software companies are under pressure to invest in new artificial intelligence capabilities without denting profits. One increasingly popular strategy to keep costs low is to shift hiring outside the US.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Bridgestone mejora las capacidades de desarrollo virtual de neumáticos con su propio simulador
item.description: Bridgestone, la compañía especializada en neumáticos y soluciones de movilidad sostenible, ha avanzado la inversión de un nuevo simulador de conducción en circuito (DiL, por sus siglas en inglés) en su centro de I+D en la región de EMEA.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Full-stack JavaScript leads the way
item.description: Building web apps begins with a simple premise, but the real-world experience is a juggling act of optimizations, best practices, toolchains, and so on. Developers tend to respond by doing what we do best: creating new tools and techniques to manage ever-changing demands.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: OpenAI’s “deep research” gives a preview of the AI agents of the future
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Zuckerberg visits White House to discuss advancing 'American tech leadership abroad': Meta
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg visited the White House on Thursday to discuss Meta's role in helping the Trump administration with America's international tech leadership, the company confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Using NATS with .NET Aspire
item.description: If you’re building a cloud-native application using a scalable cluster of microservices, you’re going to need a way for those services to communicate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google scraps its diversity hiring goals as it complies with Trump's new government contractor rules
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is scrapping some of its diversity hiring targets, joining a lengthening list of U.S. companies that have abandoned or scaled back their diversity, equity and inclusion programs.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Workday to cut 1,750 jobs, shift focus to AI and global expansion
item.description: Workday said Wednesday it will lay off 1,750 employees, roughly 8.5% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring plan to invest more heavily in artificial intelligence (AI) and accelerate international growth.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Kyndryl expands Palo Alto deal to offer managed SASE service
item.description: Kyndryl has expanded its alliance with Palo Alto Networks to add secure access service edge (SASE) services to its managed services offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: China targets Apple’s business practices; maybe the company should adapt
item.description: They say great art reflects its time. When it comes to Apple’s business, just as the iMac and iPod matched the zeitgeist, the company must now develop its business to meet the complex demands of a divided, post-globalist world struggling with economic and climate insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
